description: After the setup pain, it just works. Day-to-day tasks like patching or installing software became super simple, and I feel like I spend less time babysitting servers. It’s a solid tool if you’re already using AWS and want to keep things organized.
author: Rekha K.
pros: Thing I liked the most is that it really brings everything together and make task super easy. I can check on EC2 instances, push patches, or even handle servers outside AWS without jumping between tools. The automation they provide saves alot of time and I don't have to do all manually. Also, it plays nicely with other AWS features like Session Manager, which makes remote access way easier. The fact that most features don’t add extra cost is a big plus too.
cons: In the beginning it was little bit tough to figure out. Like you have to set up roles, permissions, and the agent, which isn’t exactly beginner-friendly. Some options are a little hidden in the interface, so I had to dig around or watch a couple of tutorials.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Once You Learn It, You’ll Wonder How You Ever Managed AWS Without It
description: Once you get over the initial setup curve, AWS Systems Manager becomes your control tower for everything AWS. It saves time, boosts security, and just makes managing infrastructure less painful. I wish I had started using it earlier, it would’ve saved a ton of late nights.
author: Tinashe N.
pros: 1. Centralized management of EC2 and hybrid servers. 2. Secure remote access through Session Manager. 3. Strong automation capabilities using Runbooks. 4. Integrates seamlessly with IAM, CloudWatch, and CloudTrail. 5. Improves visibility and compliance reporting.
cons: 1. The interface can be confusing for first-time users. 2. Documentation is thorough but not beginner-friendly. 3. Some features (like OpsCenter) require deeper AWS knowledge to configure properly.

review_title: Amazing tool, but a little complex to start with.
description: It is a powerful, indispensable, but complex tool. Once I have it set up properly, it is amazing, but getting to that point can be trying.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love being able to connect to my EC2 instances instantly via the console or CLI, completely secured by IAM policies.
cons: The sidebar is packed with too many features. It can be hard to find what I need, and it feels unintuitive for first-time users.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: AWS Systems Manager helps me easily manage my cloud resources and automate tasks.
author: Ana Z.
pros: Great management features and easy automation tools make my job easier.
cons: Some features can be complex to set up and could use more detailed documentation.

description: My overall experience with AWS system manager is really good as it’s really helpful for my business and really helps continue to grow.
author: Tristan M.
pros: What I really liked about a how easy it is to navigate as well. It wasn’t too bad when it comes to money as well having access through my other computer computers.
cons: When using AWS systems manager on putting in commands sometime, there’s a delay, which can be really inconvenient.

description: My overall experience was good and it did make the tasks a lot easier to manage and time saving which was the best part
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The best thing is the centralized management capability that we get while using AWS system manager which let us control and automate the tasks and make the work a lot easier.
cons: The thing I like least is that it sometimes have latency and delays while executing commands which can lead to slow work and negatively impact the tasks.

description: My overall experience was very satisfying, as our IT team works with different software systems, and AWS Systems Manager made it much easier to configure, monitor, and track updates across them.
author: Mehakpreet K.
pros: AWS Systems Manager was easy to install and configure, allowing smooth operations and simple management of the different configurations we used for our projects
cons: There was nothing we disliked—our team loved the interface as it was easy to handle and helped us track updates efficiently.

description: I really liked it, not many competitors' for these kind of tools, and AWS is probably the best I have used.
author: Luke T.
pros: Great for getting HPC capabilities without actually needed the physical infrastructure which is too expensive for a smaller company.
cons: Pricing can be confusing and the costs the go up quickly. There is also quite a steep learning curve.

author: Eric S.
pros: It is an overall great way to manage all your nodes in AWS. If you have enough nodes that it is cumbersome to manage them individually, Systems Manager is crucial to operations.
cons: I don't have anything negative to say about AWS Systems Manager, it is a crucial tool to managing your AWS environment.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Overall, I think that it streamlines things and boosts productivity by dramatically reducing the need to SSH into machines individually.
author: Ryan P.
pros: I think AWS Systems Manager shines in automation, visibility, and centralized control, and I like how it provides a single pane of glass to manage EC2 instances, on-prem servers and hybrid environments
cons: My least favorite thing is that the interface isn’t always intuitive. Some features are buried in menus, and documentation often requires cross-referencing multiple AWS pages.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Jesús P.
pros: Easy to use and a lot of resources available.
cons: Documentation is not easy to read. GPU instances are somwhat limited.

description: i like AWS because offering scalable resources on demand, enabling businesses to deploy applications efficiently without the need for physical servers
author: surbhi D.
pros: centralize data and automate tasks across all resources, user can create diff layers of applications. price.
cons: very complex system so not easy to learn, so many permissions and configurations. But for security.

review_title: Centralized Ops for Modern Cloud Teams
author: Niharika A.
pros: Manage EC2, hybrid servers, and resources from one place. Remotely execute scripts or commands across instances without SSH. Secure, auditable shell access without opening inbound ports. Central place for managing config data and secrets (with KMS support). Automate operational tasks and keep instances compliant. Track software, patching, and instance config state.
cons: Some features are buried or confusing for new users. Shares functionality with other AWS tools (can cause confusion). Broad toolset requires time to understand what does what. Some data (like compliance reports) updates slowly.

description: AWS Systems Manager is a powerful tool for automating and managing infrastructure, offering great visibility and control. However, the initial setup and permissions can be complex and require careful configuration.
author: Swaibu B.
pros: It can automate management tasks and securely manage instances at scale, reducing operational overhead.
cons: The complexity of initial setup and permission management can be challenging.

description: Was excited at speed of processing sign up and response yet user interface is more challenging with devices available to me
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Mostly excited about the professional appearance and services offered along with familiarity of brand name of Amazon Customer Support Will come in handy
cons: Learning Curve. I definitely have a hard time on the user interface and dashboard in order to make the most of my services
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: AWS Systems Manager makes it easy to manage, maintain, and deploy my AWS resources, saving me time and hassle.
author: Angel F.
pros: The easy-to-use interface and extensive functionality make it a valuable tool for managing AWS infrastructure.
cons: The tool can be overwhelming for new users and may require some time to fully understand and utilize all its features.

description: Overall it's been fine, but if something stops working you're in for a long fix.
author: Anthony K.
pros: It's very robust and lets you do almost whatever you want if you have the skill to implement it.
cons: It takes a high skill level to fully utilize it.

description: Overall I like how AWS housed everything
author: Olivia C.
pros: I liked that AWS hosted everything for my website and ecom business
cons: I found it so feature-rich that it was tough to initially learn

description: AWS systems manager AWS presents a simple limit, it is its prior configuration which may impose a set of services that is too complete according to your current uses, or too restricted according to your uses.
author: Mahamat seid D.
pros: I like this software because it allows me to improve visibility and control in the cloud, on premises and at the edge. This facilitates the migration process for existing applications, while preserving the options to develop new solutions.
cons: The risks of attack and loss of confidentiality are increased because depending on the system used, the company will not necessarily be able to work or dispose of its data outside the network, Returning to an “in-house” IT service is often costly and complex.

review_title: Do complex tasks just with clicks and make them easy
description: It is a big wow, because we can do some hectic tasks easily within a very short span of time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: AWS SSM provides plenty of features which reduces headache of doing complex tasks and saves a lot of time.It does offers patch manager, schedulers, automations, request for change (RFCs) and a lot lot more.
cons: It's a bit expensive but it can reduce labour and you can save money by lessen up hiring for AWS admins as it can do most of the administration tasks easily.

description: Nos brinda un gran Performance, seguridad.
author: MIGUEL M.
pros: Podemos configurar los servidores antivirus definition en un solo crm muy completo
cons: El firewall no funciona muy bien a veces tememos que reiniciar app

review_title: It was finicky and difficult to use
description: I actually ended up switching to another product that was easier to use.
author: Kelly R.
pros: It was a great place to store videos and documents that I needed for clients
cons: I found it wasn't intuitive, it was finicky and difficult to use appropriately

review_title: Seamless integration with other AWS services and Good automation tool
description: We've been using mostly in Parameter Store/Run Command/Automation for many years. Run Command/Automation is pretty good tool to maintain/deployment tasks on our ec2 instances. We could also utilize those services to create AMIs and patching systems on regular basis
author: Jitae K.
pros: Very good integration with other AWS services and can have up to 10000 standard parameters as free tier to store config data or secrets in Parameters Store. Also if you utilize or deploy any infra resources using AWS Cloudformation stack, then you can just directly access to System Manager Parameter Store Keys without exposing your values.
cons: Most painful moments in AWS System Manager is Automation documentation failures. It does not have any options starting over where it fails and so you have to start Automation documents from beginning. And if your app or webservices heavily rely on AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store, then you would see lots of api throttling errors while getting values from SSM PS.

review_title: AWS Systems Manager a helpful necessity in AWS
description: My overall experience has been great. Being able to control systems via sessions manager instead of having to use ssh keys is a critical part of our control of our environment and patch management.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The thing we like the most about AWS Systems Manager is it's ability to manage and control our instances in one place across multiple accounts.
cons: The thing I like least about AWS Systems Manager is that the amount of things that are packed into the side bar can be confusing to what is actually a core service offered by Systems manager.

description: Overall, AWS Systems Manager is a powerful and robust tool for infrastructure management and automation. While it has a learning curve, once properly configured it significantly improves operational efficiency, security, and visibility across managed resources
author: Humberto P.
pros: Centralizes management of EC2 and on-premises servers in one place. Patch Manager, Session Manager, and Automation greatly reduce manual operational work and improve security. Tight integration with other AWS services and IAM makes it reliable and scalable for enterprise environments
cons: Initial setup and configuration can be complex, especially around IAM permissions and hybrid environments. The interface is not always intuitive, and troubleshooting failures can be time-consuming due to limited or unclear error messages.
